title: Avoid Equality Checks on Floating Point Numbers
description: Floating point math is imprecise due to the challenges of storing such values in a binary representation. This rule checks for the use of direct and indirect equality/inequailty tests on floats and doubles.
recommendation: Avoid floating point comparisons. Use Java's float-handling Numbers such as BigDecimal or compare the absolute value of the difference between the stored value and the expected value against a margin of acceptable error.
impact: Improves code quality by reducing potential errors due to floating point imprecision.

checks.message: Avoid floating point comparisons. Use Java's float-handling Numbers such as BigDecimal or compare the absolute value of the difference between the stored value and the expected value against a margin of acceptable error.
